SEARCH
Package de.jvstvshd.necrify.api.duration

Interface PunishmentDuration

All Superinterfaces:
Comparable<PunishmentDuration>
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbsolutePunishmentDuration, PermanentPunishmentDuration, RelativePunishmentDuration
public interface PunishmentDuration extends Comparable<PunishmentDuration>
Class holding the duration and expiration date of a punishment.
See Also:
  • parse(String)

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    static class 
    PunishmentDuration.Parser
    A class for parsing player inputs into a valid PunishmentDuration.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    AbsolutePunishmentDuration
    absolute()
    Ensures this PunishmentDuration is absolute.
    LocalDateTime
    expiration()
    The expiration date of this duration.
    String
    expirationAsString()
    Formats the expiration date of this duration in the following format as String, if not otherwise specified: dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm:ss.
    Timestamp
    expirationAsTimestamp()
    The expiration date of this duration as Timestamp.
    static PunishmentDuration
    from(LocalDateTime ldt)
    Creates a new absolute punishment duration with the given expiration date.
    static PunishmentDuration
    fromDuration(Duration duration)
    Converts the given Duration into a PunishmentDuration.
    static PunishmentDuration
    fromMillis(long millis)
    Converts the given milliseconds into a PunishmentDuration.
    static PunishmentDuration
    fromTimestamp(Timestamp timestamp)
    Converts the given Timestamp into a PunishmentDuration.
    default PunishmentDuration
    initialDuration()
    The initial duration (before any changes to the punishment this duration was created for).
    boolean
    isPermanent()
    Whether this duration is permanent meaning the expiration should be 31.12.9999, 23:59:59.
    default Duration
    javaDuration()
    The remaining duration as a Duration object.
    static PunishmentDuration
    parse(String source)
    Parses a string to a PunishmentDuration using PunishmentDuration.Parser.
    static PunishmentDuration
    permanent()
    Creates a new permanent (expiration date: 31.12.9999, 23:59:59) absolute punishment duration.
    RelativePunishmentDuration
    relative()
    Ensures this PunishmentDuration is relative.
    String
    remainingDuration()
    Formats the remaining duration in the same format as parsed by PunishmentDuration.Parser, unless specified otherwise.

    Methods inherited from interface java.lang.Comparable

    compareTo

  • Method Details

    • parse

      static PunishmentDuration parse(String source)
      Parses a string to a PunishmentDuration using PunishmentDuration.Parser. More information about how a duration will be parsed can be found here.
      Parameters:
      source - the source string.
      Returns:
      the parsed duration
      Throws:
      PunishmentDuration.Parser.ParseException - if...
      • the source string is empty
      • the source string does not contain parsable tokens
      • the source string does not contain a unit character after a number
      • the source string contains an unknown unit character
      • the numeric value is negative
      See Also:
      • PunishmentDuration.Parser.parse()

    • permanent

      static PunishmentDuration permanent()
      Creates a new permanent (expiration date: 31.12.9999, 23:59:59) absolute punishment duration.
      Returns:
      a permanent duration

    • from

      static PunishmentDuration from(LocalDateTime ldt)
      Creates a new absolute punishment duration with the given expiration date.
      Parameters:
      ldt - the expiration date
      Returns:
      a new absolute punishment duration
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the expiration date is in the past
      Since:
      1.0.1

    • fromTimestamp

      static PunishmentDuration fromTimestamp(Timestamp timestamp)
      Converts the given Timestamp into a PunishmentDuration. The duration is absolute because it already was before.
      Parameters:
      timestamp - the timestamp which should be converted
      Returns:
      the converted duration

    • fromMillis

      static PunishmentDuration fromMillis(long millis)
      Converts the given milliseconds into a PunishmentDuration. The duration is relative the given amount into the future from a given point in time. It is absolute as soon as a punishment is enforced.
      Parameters:
      millis - how long (in milliseconds) the punishment should last
      Returns:
      the converted duration

    • fromDuration

      static PunishmentDuration fromDuration(Duration duration)
      Converts the given Duration into a PunishmentDuration. The duration is relative the given length into the future from a given point in time. It is absolute as soon as a punishment is enforced.
      Parameters:
      duration - how long the punishment should last
      Returns:
      the converted duration

    • isPermanent

      boolean isPermanent()
      Whether this duration is permanent meaning the expiration should be 31.12.9999, 23:59:59.
      Returns:
      whether this duration is permanent
      See Also:
      • PermanentPunishmentDuration.PERMANENT
      • AbsolutePunishmentDuration.MAX
      • AbsolutePunishmentDuration.MAX_TIMESTAMP

    • absolute

      AbsolutePunishmentDuration absolute()
      Ensures this PunishmentDuration is absolute. If it is already absolute, this instance will be returned. If it is relative, it will be converted to an absolute duration.
      Returns:
      an absolute duration
      See Also:
      • AbsolutePunishmentDuration

    • relative

      RelativePunishmentDuration relative()
      Ensures this PunishmentDuration is relative. If it is already relative, this instance will be returned. If it is absolute, it will be converted to a relative duration.
      Returns:
      a relative duration
      See Also:
      • RelativePunishmentDuration

    • expiration

      LocalDateTime expiration()
      The expiration date of this duration.
      Returns:
      the expiration date

    • expirationAsTimestamp

      Timestamp expirationAsTimestamp()
      The expiration date of this duration as Timestamp.
      Returns:
      the duration
      Since:
      1.0.1

    • expirationAsString

      String expirationAsString()
      Formats the expiration date of this duration in the following format as String, if not otherwise specified: dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm:ss.
      Returns:
      the end of this punishment as string
      Since:
      1.0.1

    • remainingDuration

      String remainingDuration()
      Formats the remaining duration in the same format as parsed by PunishmentDuration.Parser, unless specified otherwise.
      Returns:
      the remaining duration

    • initialDuration

      default PunishmentDuration initialDuration()
      The initial duration (before any changes to the punishment this duration was created for).
      Returns:
      the initial duration
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - default; if this method is not implemented by its underlying implementation
      Since:
      1.0.1

    • javaDuration

      default Duration javaDuration()
      The remaining duration as a Duration object.
      Returns:
      the remaining duration
      Since:
      1.2.0
      See Also:
      • Duration.between(Temporal, Temporal)
